title: monocotyledonae
intro: The rank of Monocotyledonae refers to a group of flowering plants characterized by the presence of one cotyledon or seed leaf in their seeds. This group comprises a diverse range of plant orders, including Asparagales, which includes asparagus, orchids, and hyacinths. The order Malpighiales, on the other hand, encompasses a variety of plants, such as mangroves and willows, which are commonly found in wetland ecosystems. Additionally, the order Poales includes plants like grasses, sedges, and rushes, which are often found in grasslands and wet environments.

orders.order_desc: Poisales is an order of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes.
